title: silene mollissima subsp. gibraltarica
intro: Silene mollissima subsp. gibraltarica is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, native to Gibraltar and the Iberian Peninsula. It is also known as Gibraltar Catchfly, a biennial or perennial plant with white or pink flowers. The medicinal properties of Silene mollissima subsp. gibraltarica include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent, which can help soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions, tinctures, or salves, can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, offering benefits like improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function.
